input.background: The case-control study on esophageal cancer and alcohol use also collected data on tobacco use as a potential risk factor, with tobacco use dichotomized at 20 grams/day.
input.question: Calculate the odds ratio and 95% confidence interval for these data.

output.answer: Odds ratio = 1.96, 95% confidence for the OR = (1.39 to 2.77)

Tobacco_use.description: Categorical variable indicating the level of tobacco use, dichotomized at 20 grams/day.

Cases.description: Numerical variable representing the number of cases of esophageal cancer for each level of tobacco use.

Noncases.description: Numerical variable representing the number of non-cases (controls) for each level of tobacco use.
